hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: Who Am I Professionally -- The Zurich Interview, Round 1
lesson.menu_desc: A identidade profissional em inglês: sete palavras do setor de seguros e o verbo to be nas três formas (I am / I am not / Are you?) -- até a frase que conta a transição de carreira: "I am not a broker -- I am a commercial professional." -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- Who Am I Professionally
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 2 -- Who Am I Professionally
lesson.grammar_point: verb to be -- 1st and 2nd person (I am / I am not / Are you?)

listenings.text: Hello, Paula. This is Mark, from Zurich. I am the commercial director here. Our interview is on Friday, at three o'clock. It is a short interview — thirty minutes. And please relax: I am not an English teacher. Your English is not the job. The job is insurance, and you are an insurance professional. That is what I want to hear about. Are you free at three? Please confirm. Thank you. Goodbye.

Good morning, everybody. For our new colleague: my name is Sofia. I am the commercial manager for the south region. I am not a broker — I am on the company side, like you. Our clients this month are two banks and a factory. Paula, you are new here, and I am your first contact. Are you free on Tuesday? My office is on the third floor. Welcome to the team.

paras: Sofia Reyes is a commercial manager at Zurich. Zurich is an insurance company. Sofia is from Lima, in Peru. She is in São Paulo now. She is not a broker. She works with brokers, every day.

This is Sofia, in her own words: "Hello! My name is Sofia. I am forty-one years old. I am a commercial professional. I am not a broker — I am on the company side. My clients are big companies: banks, factories, hospitals."

"Is my English perfect? No, it is not. My first language is Spanish. Nobody here is perfect in English — we are an international company, and that is normal. Are you new here? Come and say hello. I am always happy to meet a new colleague."

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Sofia is a broker, and she sells insurance to big companies.
Sofia is a commercial professional at Zurich — and her English is not perfect, and that is not a problem.
Sofia is an English teacher at an international company.

items: She is not. The text says: "She is not a broker. She works with brokers, every day."
The first paragraph says it directly: "Zurich is an insurance company."
"Sofia is from Lima, in Peru." She is in São Paulo now.
It is Spanish. She says: "My first language is Spanish."
Sofia thinks her English is a problem at work.
No. She says: "Nobody here is perfect in English — we are an international company, and that is normal."

bank.label: Your professional introduction / A sua apresentação profissional
items: My name is Paula. Please call me Paula.
I am not a broker — I am on the company side now.

items.situation: The interviewer asks: "Are you a broker?" You were a broker for twenty years — but not anymore. Answer.
tips: I am on the company side now.

items.situation: "What do you do?" Say what you ARE, not only where you work.
"Are you a manager?" You are not a manager yet. Answer honestly, with a full sentence.
A new colleague asks: "Who are your clients?" Say what kind of companies.
The person thinks you are a broker. Correct it: say what you are NOT, then what you ARE.
tips: I am not a broker. I am a commercial professional.
I am on the company side now.

items.situation: You want to know the other person's job. Ask a question with the verb "to be".
tips: Are you a manager? / Are you new here?
